sentences.sentence: Day after day, we hear about how anthropogenic development is causing global warming.
sentences.translation: 日复一日，我们都听到人类活动的发展正在导致全球变暖。

structure.text: Day after day
we hear about
how anthropogenic development is causing global warming

difficult_points: Day after day 强调这一说法反复出现。
how 引导 hear about 的宾语从句；anthropogenic 表示由人类活动造成的。

sentences.sentence: According to an increasingly vocal minority, however, we should be asking ourselves how much of this is media hype, and how much is based on real evidence.
sentences.translation: 然而，按照一个声音越来越大的少数群体的说法，我们应该反问自己：其中有多少是媒体炒作，又有多少建立在真实证据之上。

structure.text: According to an increasingly vocal minority
however
we should be asking ourselves
how much of this is media hype, and how much is based on real evidence

difficult_points: vocal minority 指人数不多但公开发声积极的一群人。
两个 how much 从句并列，分别衡量媒体炒作与真实证据的比例。

expressions.meaning_zh: 声音越来越大的少数群体

sentences.sentence: It seems – as so often is the ease – that it depends on which expert you listen to, or which statistics you study.
sentences.translation: 看来——通常情况正是如此——结论取决于你听哪位专家的意见，或研究哪一组统计数据。

structure.text: It seems
as so often is the ease
that it depends on which expert you listen to, or which statistics you study

difficult_points: 第一个 It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语。
which expert... 与 which statistics... 是并列宾语从句。
as so often is the ease 很可能应为 as so often is the case，源文错误按原样保留。

sentences.sentence: Yes, it is true that there is a mass of evidence to indicate that the world is getting warmer, with one of the world's leading weather predictors stating that air temperatures have frown an increase of just under half a degree Celsius since the beginning of the twentieth century.
sentences.translation: 诚然，大量证据表明全球正在变暖；世界顶尖气象预测专家之一指出，自20世纪初以来，气温升幅略低于0.5摄氏度。

structure.text: it is true
that there is a mass of evidence to indicate that the world is getting warmer
with one of the world's leading weather predictors stating that air temperatures have frown an increase of just under half a degree Celsius since the beginning of the twentieth century

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，that 从句说明被承认的事实。
with 复合结构补充一项专家证据，stating 后又接 that 从句。
have frown an increase 是源文异常，可能原意为 have shown an increase，按原文保留。

expressions.text: just under half a degree Celsius

sentences.sentence: And while this may not sound like anything worth losing sleep over, the international press would have us believe that the consequences could be devastating.
sentences.translation: 尽管这听起来可能不值得让人夜不能寐，国际媒体却试图让我们相信其后果可能是毁灭性的。

structure.text: while this may not sound like anything worth losing sleep over
the international press
would have us believe
that the consequences could be devastating

difficult_points: while 在此引导让步从句。
worth losing sleep over 修饰 anything；have us believe 是使役结构。

sentences.sentence: Other experts, however, are of the opinion that what we are seeing is just part of a natural upward and downward swing that has always been part of the cycle of global weather.
sentences.translation: 然而，另一些专家认为，我们正在看到的现象只是自然升降波动的一部分，而这种波动一直是全球气候循环的一部分。

structure.text: Other experts, however, are of the opinion
that what we are seeing is just part of a natural upward and downward swing
that has always been part of the cycle of global weather

difficult_points: 第一个 that 从句说明 opinion 的内容，其中 what we are seeing 作主语。
第二个 that 修饰 swing，说明这种波动一直存在于气候周期。

sentences.sentence: An analysis of the views of major meteorologists in the United States showed that less than 20% of them believed that any change in temperature over the last hundred years was our own fault – the rest attributed it to natural cyclical changes.
sentences.translation: 对美国主要气象学家观点的分析显示，不到20%的人认为过去一百年的气温变化是人类自身造成的，其余人则把这种变化归因于自然周期性变化。

structure.text: An analysis of the views of major meteorologists in the United States showed
that less than 20% of them believed that any change in temperature over the last hundred years was our own fault
the rest attributed it to natural cyclical changes

difficult_points: them 指 major meteorologists；believed 后还有一层 that 宾语从句。
the rest 指其余80%以上专家，it 指温度变化。

sentences.sentence: There is, of course, no denying that we are still at a very early stage in understanding weather.
sentences.translation: 当然，不可否认的是，我们对天气的理解仍处于非常早期的阶段。

structure.text: There is
of course
no denying
that we are still at a very early stage in understanding weather

difficult_points: There is no denying that 是固定结构，表示不可否认。
at a very early stage in doing 表示某项认识尚处早期。

expressions.text: at a very early stage in

sentences.sentence: The effects of such variables as rainfall, cloud formation, the seas and oceans, gases such as methane and ozone, or even solar energy are still not really understood, and therefore the predictions that we make using them cannot always be relied on.
sentences.translation: 降雨、云层形成、海洋、甲烷和臭氧等气体，甚至太阳能量这些变量的影响仍未被真正理解，因此我们利用它们作出的预测并非总是可靠。

structure.text: The effects of such variables as rainfall, cloud formation, the seas and oceans, gases such as methane and ozone, or even solar energy are still not really understood
and therefore the predictions that we make using them cannot always be relied on

difficult_points: such variables as 后列出多个影响天气的变量。
that 修饰 predictions，using them 是方式状语，them 指前述 variables。
rely on 的介词保留在被动结构末尾。

sentences.sentence: Dr James Hansen, in 19BH, was predicting that the likely effects of global warming would be a raising of the world temperature which would have disastrous consequences for mankind: "a strong cause arid effect relationship between the current climate and human alteration of the atmosphere".
sentences.translation: 詹姆斯·汉森博士在原文所写的“19BH”年曾预测，全球变暖可能使全球气温上升，并给人类带来灾难性后果，即“当前气候与人类改变大气之间存在强烈的因果关系”。

structure.text: Dr James Hansen, in 19BH, was predicting
that the likely effects of global warming would be a raising of the world temperature
which would have disastrous consequences for mankind
a strong cause arid effect relationship between the current climate and human alteration of the atmosphere

difficult_points: which 更自然地指 temperature raising 这一结果。
冒号后引语补充汉森当时的核心判断。
19BH、a raising 和 cause arid effect 均为源文异常，可能分别对应年份、a rise、cause and effect，按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: He has now gone on record as stating that using artificial models of climate as a way of predicting change is all but impossible.
sentences.translation: 如今他已公开表示，利用人工气候模型预测变化几乎是不可能的。

structure.text: He has now gone on record as stating
that using artificial models of climate as a way of predicting change is all but impossible

difficult_points: go on record as doing 表示公开、正式表明立场。
using... 是 that 从句的动名词主语；all but 意为几乎、差一点就是。

sentences.sentence: In fact, he now believes that, rather than getting hotter, our planet is getting greener as a result of the carbon dioxide increase, with the prospect of increasing vegetation in areas which in recent history have been frozen wastelands.
sentences.translation: 事实上，他现在认为，地球并非变得更热，而是因二氧化碳增加而变得更绿，并且那些近代曾是冰冻荒原的地区可能会增加植被。

structure.text: In fact, he now believes
that, rather than getting hotter, our planet is getting greener as a result of the carbon dioxide increase
with the prospect of increasing vegetation in areas which in recent history have been frozen wastelands

difficult_points: rather than 对比 getting hotter 与 getting greener。
with the prospect of... 补充可能结果；which 修饰 areas。

sentences.sentence: In fact, there is some evidence to suggest that as our computer-based weather models have become more sophisticated, the predicted rises In temperature have been cut back.
sentences.translation: 事实上，有证据表明，随着计算机天气模型日益复杂精密，预测的气温升幅已经被下调。

structure.text: In fact, there is some evidence
to suggest
that as our computer-based weather models have become more sophisticated, the predicted rises In temperature have been cut back

difficult_points: to suggest 修饰 evidence，that 从句说明证据所指内容。
as 表示随着模型进步；have been cut back 是现在完成时被动。
rises In 中 In 异常大写，按源文保留。

sentences.sentence: In addition, if we look at the much-reported rise in global temperature over the last century, a close analysis reveals that the lion's share of that increase, almost three quarters in total, occurred before man began to "poison" his world with industrial processes and the accompanying greenhouse gas emissions in the second half of the twentieth century.
sentences.translation: 此外，如果审视上世纪广为报道的全球升温，仔细分析会发现，其中绝大部分——总计近四分之三——发生在人类于20世纪下半叶开始通过工业生产及伴随的温室气体排放“污染”世界之前。

structure.text: if we look at the much-reported rise in global temperature over the last century
a close analysis reveals
that the lion's share of that increase, almost three quarters in total, occurred
before man began to "poison" his world with industrial processes and the accompanying greenhouse gas emissions in the second half of the twentieth century

difficult_points: the lion's share 是习语，指最大部分；almost three quarters 是同位补充。
before 从句说明大部分升温发生的时间早于大规模工业排放。

sentences.sentence: So should we pay any attention to those stories that scream out at us from billboards and television news headlines, claiming that man, with his inexhaustible dependence on oil-based machinery and ever more sophisticated forms of transport is creating a nightmare level of greenhouse gas emissions, poisoning his environment and ripping open the ozone layer?
sentences.translation: 那么，我们是否应理会那些从广告牌和电视新闻标题中向我们大声叫喊的报道？它们声称，人类对石油机械和日益先进交通工具的无尽依赖，正在制造噩梦般的温室气体排放，污染环境并撕裂臭氧层。

structure.text: So should we pay any attention to those stories
that scream out at us from billboards and television news headlines

claiming that man, with his inexhaustible dependence on oil-based machinery and ever more sophisticated forms of transport is creating a nightmare level of greenhouse gas emissions, poisoning his environment and ripping open the ozone layer

difficult_points: that 修饰 stories，scream out 是拟人表达。
claiming 的逻辑主语是 stories，其后 that 从句内容很长。
with... 是插入背景，poisoning 与 ripping 是伴随结果；transport 后缺逗号是源文异常。

sentences.sentence: Doubters point to scientific evidence, which can prove that, of all the greenhouse gases, only two per cent come from man-made sources, the rest resulting from natural emissions.
sentences.translation: 怀疑者援引科学证据，称在所有温室气体中，只有2%来自人为来源，其余则来自自然排放。

structure.text: Doubters point to scientific evidence
which can prove that, of all the greenhouse gases, only two per cent come from man-made sources
the rest resulting from natural emissions

difficult_points: which 指 scientific evidence，that 从句说明其证明的内容。
the rest resulting... 是独立主格结构，resulting 前省略 are。

sentences.sentence: Who, then, to believe: the environmentalist exhorting us to leave the car at home, to buy re-usable products packaged in recycled paper and to plant trees in our back yard?
sentences.translation: 那么，该相信谁呢：是那位敦促我们把车留在家中、购买用再生纸包装的可重复使用产品，并在后院种树的环保主义者吗？

structure.text: Who, then, to believe
the environmentalist exhorting us
to leave the car at home, to buy re-usable products packaged in recycled paper and to plant trees in our back yard

difficult_points: Who to believe 是省略主语和助动词的“疑问词＋不定式”问句。
exhorting... 修饰 environmentalist，三个 to do 是 exhort 的并列宾补。

sentences.sentence: Or the sceptics, including, of course, a lot of big businesses who have most to lose, when they tell us that we are making a mountain out of a molehill?
sentences.translation: 还是该相信那些怀疑者——其中当然包括许多损失风险最大的企业——在他们告诉我们这是小题大做时所说的话呢？

structure.text: Or the sceptics
including, of course, a lot of big businesses who have most to lose
when they tell us that we are making a mountain out of a molehill

difficult_points: 本句承接上句 Who to believe，省略完整问句主干。
who 修饰 big businesses；they 指 sceptics。
make a mountain out of a molehill 是习语，表示夸大微小问题。

expressions.text: make a mountain out of a molehill

sentences.sentence: And my own opinion?
sentences.translation: 那么我自己的看法呢？

structure.text: And
my own opinion

difficult_points: 这是省略谓语的简短问句，完整含义是“我的观点是什么”。
And 用于从两派观点转向作者自己的立场。

sentences.sentence: The jury's still out as for as I am concerned!
sentences.translation: 就我而言，现在还没有定论！

structure.text: The jury's still out
as for as I am concerned

difficult_points: the jury is still out 是习语，表示尚无定论。
as for as 很可能应为 as far as，是源文拼写异常，按原文保留。

expressions.text: as far as I am concerned
